data.title: Management Works to Insulate Albemarle From 'Increased Spot Market Risk,' Says Oppenheimer
data.description:
Albemarle is facing "increased spot market risk" and its management has been building the company's resilience for it, Oppenheimer said in a Friday note.

"We are encouraged by the prospect of Albemarle being free cash flow positive in 2025, despite a higher percentage of spot sales and continued anemic spot prices," the investment firm said. According to Oppenheimer, Albemarle's cost cuts and spot prices' recovery from Q3 lows led to its improved Q4 gross margin.

Oppenheimer also said it expects lithium demand to remain robust, but sees a U-shaped recovery for prices as the sector halts capacity expansion with the backdrop of flat to higher in the US and the European Union and growing electric vehicle sales in China.

Oppenheimer lowered its price target on Albemarle's stock to $123, from $170 due to a cautious view on 2025 and 2026 pricing, while reiterating an outperform rating.

data.title: Apple Restores TikTok to App Store. How It Could Help the iPhone Maker. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Adam Clark

Apple and Google have put TikTok back on their mobile app stores. It's the latest effort by the Trump administration to keep the video-sharing app operating in the U.S.

Apple and Google received letters from U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi assuring them they wouldn't face legal consequences if they put the app back in their stores to download, The Wall Street Journal reported, citing people familiar with the matter. It had been removed to comply with a U.S. law that requires TikTok's Chinese parent, ByteDance, to sell the app or shut it down.

President Donald Trump signed an executive order on the day of his inauguration delaying enforcement of the law. In regard to TikTok's ownership, he has suggested interest from such companies as Oracle, Microsoft, and Amazon.com in its U.S. business.

For Apple, the reassurance that it won't face legal risks over making the TikTok app available comes at a delicate time in its own negotiations with China. Apple is aiming to get Chinese regulatory approval for the launch of artificial-intelligence features on its devices in the country.

Apple is now aiming to launch AI features in China by the middle of this year in partnership with Alibaba and Baidu, with models that will censor their output to comply with Beijing's requirements, Bloomberg reported on Friday.

"The China launch now looks set for early May which is key to the long awaited growth catalyst in this region with our estimates that over 100 million of the roughly 200 million iPhones in China are in the window of an upgrade opportunity," wrote Wedbush analyst Daniel Ives in a research note.

Ives has an Outperform rating and $325 target price on Apple stock.

Apple recently lost its No. 1 spot in smartphone market share in China as domestic rivals took advantage of the iPhone maker's lack of AI features on phones purchased in the country, according to data from research firm Canalys.

Analysts at KeyBanc reiterated an Underweight rating on Apple stock with a $200 target price in a research note on Thursday, citing expectations of just 0.1% iPhone revenue growth in fiscal 2025 in its fiscal year 2025, amid tougher competition in China, and its premium valuation.

Apple shares were up 0.8% at $243.49 in morning trading.

Write to Adam Clark at adam.clark@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Springfield, Missouri Selling $275.5M in Securities for Power Generation, Battery Storage
data.description:
By Zaeem Shoaib

Springfield, Missouri's Board of Public Utilities plans to sell $275.5 million of securities to pay for the construction of three 50MW combustion turbines at McCartney Generating Station and acquiring utility scale zinc battery storage.

Funds from the issuance will also be used for improvements to the public utility system as part of the city's capital improvement plan, according to documents posted Thursday on MuniOS.

The Certificates of Participation, Series 2025, that are due Nov. 1 from 2028 through 2047, with interest payable semiannually.

Pricing of the securities is expected on Feb. 26, with closing expected on March 12.

The securities are payable solely from revenues from the city's public utility system, which includes electric, natural gas, water, telecommunications/broadband and transit departments and certain other minor services.

The total cost for the turbines and battery storage is expected to be about $285 million, of which $280 million will be financed with proceeds of the certificates. The additional $5 million of costs will be financed with internally generated funds.

Springfield's capital improvement plan for the public utility system for the next five years includes new construction, improvements, renewals and acquisitions related to power generation, electricity, natural gas, water supply and distribution, transportation and telecommunications/broadband services.

Costs for the capital improvement plan are expected to be $93.7 million in 2025, $80.7 million in 2026, $71.8 million in 2027, $76.1 million in 2028 and $64.4 million in 2029.

S&P Global Ratings and Fitch Ratings have each assigned AA ratings to the bonds.

Write to Zaeem Shoaib at zaeem.shoaib@wsj.com

data.title: InflaRx Shares Drop After Public Offering Prices
data.description:
By Chris Wack

InflaRx shares dropped 29%, to $1.92, after the company priced an underwritten public offering of 8.3 million shares at $2 a share, and in lieu of shares to certain investors, pre-funded warrants to buy up to 6.75 million shares.

All ordinary shares and pre-funded warrants in the offering are being sold by the company.

The offering is expected to close Tuesday.

InflaRx has granted the underwriters a 30-day option to buy up to 2.25 million additional shares at the public offering price, less the underwriting discount.

The company intends to use the proceeds from the offering primarily to fund clinical development of its pipeline candidates, including vilobelimab and INF904, and for general corporate purposes.

Write to Chris Wack at chris.wack@wsj.com

data.title: Has Ethereum lost its edge? Experts weigh in
data.description:
The Ethereum blockchain has been facing a tough time in 2025. Despite the bull market, its native token, Ether (ETH), remains well below its all-time highs — and its value relative to Bitcoin (BTC) has been in steady decline for over three years. As competition among layer-1 networks heats up and sentiment soars, many are wondering: Have Ethereum’s best days already passed?

The Ethereum network has been the backbone of decentralized applications, smart contracts and decentralized finance (DeFi). However, newer blockchains are emerging as serious contenders, with some even surpassing Ethereum in key areas. Issues like economic sustainability and usability problems have led to increased doubts about the network’s future.

Cointelegraph recently interviewed top experts and analysts to better understand what’s happening with Ethereum. While it still enjoys the first-mover advantage, the growing competition, combined with internal challenges, places some questions over its long-term viability. Can Ethereum survive its struggles, or is it being left behind?

To find out more about Ethereum’s current state, the problems it faces and experts’ insights on what could spark a turnaround, watch the full video on our YouTube channel, and don’t forget to subscribe!

Related: Vitalik argues for even higher ETH gas limit

data.title: From Empires to Collectors: Apollo Art Auctions Presents Ancient Art & Antiquities February e-Sale
data.description:
LONDON, Feb. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Apollo Art Auctions is pleased to announce its Ancient Art & Antiquities February e-sale, a two-day online event featuring a carefully curated selection of ancient artefacts and coins. The auction will take place on February 22 and 23, 2025, starting at 13:00 GMT each day. The sale includes items from Classical Europe, Egypt, the Near East, and beyond, with each piece authenticated by experts.

A Media Snippet accompanying this announcement is available by clicking on this link.

Featured Items in the Auction

The auction includes a diverse range of artefacts, offering collectors and enthusiasts the opportunity to acquire significant pieces of history. Some highlights include:

Egyptian Wooden Ba Bird Statuette (Lot 29)

A wooden depiction of the Ba bird, symbolizing the soul in ancient Egyptian beliefs, dating to the Late Period.

Estimate: £3,000 - £5,000

Roman Gold Ring with Minerva and Snake Intaglio (Lot 92)

A Roman gold ring with an intaglio carving of the goddess Minerva, the goddess of wisdom, accompanied by a coiled snake.

Estimate: £4,000 - £6,000

Roman Bronze Gorgon Phalera Inlaid with Silver (Lot 97)

A Roman bronze phalera featuring a Gorgon, likely used as a military decoration. The piece is notable for its fine craftsmanship.

Estimate: £6,000 - £8,000

Gandhara Schist Stone Figure of Standing Buddha (Lot 373)

A finely carved schist figure from the Gandhara region, reflecting the unique blend of Hellenistic and Buddhist artistic influences.

Estimate: £7,000 - £12,000

Day 2: More Notable Pieces

On Day 2 (February 23, 2025), several additional important items will be featured, including:

Egyptian Wooden Boat Model (Lot 401)

A wooden model of a boat, used in Egyptian tombs as a symbol of the journey to the afterlife.

Estimate: £15,000 - £20,000

Apulian Red-Figure Bell Krater with Lady of Fashion (Lot 450)

A red-figure bell krater featuring the elegant ‘Lady of Fashion’ motif, a hallmark of Apulian pottery.

Estimate: £6,000 - £9,000

Pair of Hellenistic Gold Earrings with Agate Beads (Lot 458)

A set of gold earrings from the Hellenistic period, adorned with agate beads.

Estimate: £10,000 - £15,000

Roman Gold Ring with Sapphire Intaglio Depicting Butterfly (Lot 467)

A Roman gold ring featuring a finely carved sapphire intaglio of a butterfly, a common symbol of the soul.

Estimate: £5,000 - £7,000

Auction Details

  • Day 1: February 22, 2025, 13:00 GMT
  • Day 2: February 23, 2025, 13:00 GMT
  • Online Auction: auction.apolloauctions.com
  • Payment Options: USD, GBP, EUR
  • Shipping: In-house packing and shipping services available

For more information or to request condition reports, please contact enquiries@apolloauctions.com.

About Apollo Art Auctions

Founded in 2010 by Dr. Ivan Bonchev, Apollo Art Auctions specializes in ancient art, antiquities, and coins, including Ancient, Asian, and Islamic art. Based in central London (WC1), we serve a global audience, offering expertly vetted and catalogued artefacts.

With 20,000 clients across 70+ countries, we are a trusted leader in antiquities and pre-16th century art. We provide free valuations and a seamless experience for buyers and sellers worldwide.

data.title: Cadence Design Q4 Expected to Meet Guidance Amid AI Growth Uncertainty, Oppenheimer Says
data.description:
Cadence Design Systems Q4 results are expected to align with prior guidance, with a strong bookings pipeline for the quarter, but stock reactions could be mixed, Oppenheimer said in an earnings preview Friday.

Bookings are anticipated to reach record levels, though quarterly growth may be uneven due to upfront revenue recognition. While the company has consistently exceeded revenue guidance in the past 12 quarters, stock reactions have been mixed, rising only about 40% of the time, according to the note.

The company's AI-driven growth remains elusive, with limited revenue disclosures, and faces a long-term risk as it focuses on AI accelerators while competing with more efficient, faster, and cheaper AI-native software, Oppenheimer said.

Insider selling of Cadence stock also gives mixed signals to the market regarding the company's AI-focused growth path, with the CEO recently selling 60% of his stake and insiders offloading $362 million in shares since the GenAI boom began, the firm added.

Oppenheimer reiterated its underperform rating on Cadence and maintained its $225 price target.

data.title: Market Chatter: Diamondback Energy in Talks to Acquire Double Eagle
data.description:
Diamondback Energy is in talks to buy Double Eagle in a transaction potentially valuing the business at over $5 billion, The Wall Street Journal reported Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.

A deal could be announced in the coming days unless negotiations fail or another suitor comes along, according to the report.

Double Eagle, a Permian Basin producer with more than 95,000 net acres in the Midland Basin, is backed by private equity firm EnCap Investments.

Diamondback Energy, Double Eagle, and EnCap Investments did not immediately respond to requests for comment from MT Newswires.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Zus Health Celebrates Unprecedented Growth, Announcing New Clients and AI-Powered Innovations
data.description:
BOSTON, Feb. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Zus Health is proud to announce a transformative milestone in its journey to reshape care delivery through data interoperability and actionable insights. Over the past year, Zus has grown its revenue eightfold, with 12 new client partnerships signed in the last quarter alone, further solidifying its position as a critical player in advancing value-based care. With over 60 live customers and cutting-edge AI-powered innovations, Zus has truly arrived as a driving force in healthcare transformation.

These new partnerships include leading organizations like Cityblock, DispatchHealth, and more. Representing a range of care models and specialties, these clients are leveraging Zus’s ability to provide a unified, actionable view of patient data to improve outcomes. Early adopters have already integrated seamlessly and report high satisfaction.

Zus’s growth is powered by its commitment to product innovation and scalability, particularly through its expanding suite of AI-driven capabilities. First up, our newly released human-readable narrative summaries transform unstructured clinical data into clear, concise insights—giving care teams instant access to the information they need while preserving accuracy and source transparency. We’re also introducing an AI-driven risk suspecting engine, which surfaces hidden risk factors by incorporating clinical data beyond traditional practice settings, improving the accuracy and speed of risk coding.

By focusing on high-impact conditions and tailoring solutions to customer-specific priorities, Zus is delivering tools to meet the evolving needs of value-based care organizations. Future enhancements, already being tested with users, promise to further refine the identification of care gaps and deliver even greater insights.

As Zus Health’s CEO Jonathan Bush explains, “Healthcare has been stuck in the slow lane for far too long. At Zus, we’re not just speeding it up—we’re rewiring it for good. With new partnerships, rapidly evolving AI tools, and a growing client base, we’re proving what’s possible when healthcare providers have the right tools and information at their fingertips.”

The numbers tell a compelling story. Over the past year, revenue has grown more than eightfold. Monthly billable patients have grown fivefold in the past year. Zus is fully integrated with existing EHR platforms, including Canvas, Elation, Healthie, and more, allowing customers to seamlessly connect and enhance their workflows. We are actively continuing to expand our integration network to support even more partners this year.

This growth has been underpinned by the trust and satisfaction of our customers. With over 60 live clients, including HarmonyCares, Firefly Health, and Author Health, to name a few, Zus has become synonymous with simplifying data aggregation and improving care delivery. And in a recent testimonial video, Ben Hall, SVP of Product and Technology from Author Health described Zus as the “easy button” for integrating actionable data into workflows, a sentiment echoed across the client base.

Zus Health’s mission to give providers clinical truth in the most places, in one clear voice, is driving the transformation of care delivery. By bringing information speed to healthcare, Zus is not only meeting today’s challenges but paving the way for a more efficient, effective future.

About Zus Health

Zus Health is transforming healthcare by creating a single, always-on source of truth in patient data. Founded in 2021 by Jonathan Bush, co-founder and former CEO of athenahealth, Zus bridges data across care settings, empowering providers with actionable insights to improve patient outcomes and operational performance. Guided by its mission to give providers clinical truth in the most places, in one clear voice, Zus enables care delivery organizations to enhance coordination, improve workflows, and drive sustainable improvements in healthcare. To learn more, visit www.zushealth.com.

Media Contact:

Darren Brandt

Co-CEO, Sloane & Company

dbrandt@sloanepr.com

(212) 446-1861

data.title: Udemy Shares Touch 52-Week High After Narrower-Than-Expected 4Q Loss
data.description:
By Chris Wack

Shares of Udemy hit a 52-week high after the company reported a fourth-quarter loss that was narrower than analysts expected.

The stock was up as much as $14.24 in early Friday trading. Shares were recently 33% higher, at $10.40, but are still down 26% in the past 12 months.

The packaged-software company on Thursday reported a quarterly loss of $10 million, or 7 cents a share, compared with a loss of $20.2 million, or 13 cents a share, in the same quarter last year. Analysts polled by FactSet were looking for a loss of $16.6 million, or 11 cents a share.

Revenue for the quarter was $199.9 million, compared with the $194.7 million analysts were expecting.

Udemy said its Enterprise segment had revenue of $130.1 million, up 13% year over year. But its consumer segment had revenue of $69.8 million, down 7%. The company said changes in foreign-exchange rates hurt the segment's revenue by two percentage points.

For the first quarter, the San Francisco company is expecting revenue of $195 million to $199 million, while analysts are expecting revenue of $197.7 million. For 2025, the company is expecting revenue of $787 million to $803 million, while analysts are forecasting revenue of $796.6 million.

Write to Chris Wack at chris.wack@wsj.com

data.title: Toronto Stocks Retreat; CAE Shares Rise on New Board Appointments
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

Toronto indexes were firmly lower Friday mid-trading, dragged down by most sectors trending lower. Materials, consumer services and health-tech stocks were the main drags of the session, with only consumer service posting any notable gains, followed more distantly by health services and tech stocks.

Canadian manufacturing activity continued to pick up into the end of last year, rising 0.3% in December, thanks to a drive in sales from a weakening Canadian dollar and better petroleum trade.

Canada's S&P/TSX Composite Index fell 0.4% to 25596.3 and blue-chip S&P/TSX 60 declined by 0.5% to 1539.44.

Shares of CAE rose nearly 17% to 38.91 Canadian dollars ($27.41) after the flight-training company made changes to its board, appointing four new directors, including Air Canada's former CEO as chair of the board, while it searches for its next chief executive.

Other market movers:

Shares in Magna International fell by 4.2% to C$53.88 after the auto-parts maker said it expects lower sales over the next two years, below analyst forecasts of growth, as the auto industry faces challenges and uncertainty.

Enbridge shares fell 3% to C$61.74 after the pipeline operator and gas utility reported a slump in earnings for the latest quarter as it was squeezed by accounting changes on derivative instruments.

Shares of TC Energy fell by 3.1% to C$65.11. The company bumped up its quarterly dividend in the latest quarter in anticipation of a further rise in underlying earnings this year and after it spinned off its liquids pipelines business.

Shares of MTY Food Group fell 9.1% to C$45.50 after the company reported a loss in the fourth quarter due to an impairment charge on one of its brand assets as well as large foreign-exchange losses.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com

data.title: These Stocks Are Moving the Most Today: Super Micro, Coinbase, SoundHound, Airbnb, Moderna, Intel, DaVita, GameStop, and More — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Joe Woelfel

Stocks traded slightly to the upside Friday after President Donald Trump held off on immediately levying reciprocal tariffs on U.S. trading partners. Meanwhile, a decline in U.S. retail sales for January was wider than expected.

These stocks were moving Friday:

Intel fell 4%, putting the chip maker's winning streak in jeopardy. The stock closed Thursday at $24.13, up 7.3%, rising for four straight days and gaining 26% over the period. It was the best four-day stretch for the stock since the four days ended Nov. 2, 1987, when it rose 38%, according to Dow Jones Market Data. The rally this week received a boost Tuesday when Vice President JD Vance said at the AI Action Summit in Paris that the Trump administration "will ensure that the most powerful AI systems are built in the U.S. with American designed and manufactured chips."

Super Micro Computer jumped 11%. Shares of the server maker have risen for three straight sessions and the stock has risen 22% over the period, according to Dow Jones Market Data. The company issued a fiscal second-quarter business update Tuesday. While its near-term revenue outlook was a bit soft, longer-term sales guidance was well above analysts' expectations.

Airbnb was profitable in the fourth quarter and the shares rallied 15% even as the short-term stay company forecast revenue in 2025 that was slightly below Wall Street expectations. Airbnb earned 73 cents a share in the fourth quarter, compared with a year-earlier loss of 55 cents, as revenue rose 12% to $2.48 billion and topped estimates. Gross booking value in the period rose 13% to $17.6 billion. Airbnb said it anticipates first-quarter revenue of $2.23 billion to $2.27 billion, below analysts' expectations of $2.29 billion.

Moderna fell 1.2%. The vaccine maker reported fourth-quarter revenue of $1 billion, compared with the consensus estimate of $943 million. The company lost $2.91 a share in the quarter, while analysts had called for a loss of $2.68. Moderna pre-reported its annual earnings and previewed its 2025 guidance in mid-January; the stock fell 17% that day.

SoundHound AI sank 25% after Nvidia's latest 13-F filing revealed the chip giant no longer held a position in the maker of voice-based artificial-intelligence technology. U.S.-listed shares of Arm Holdings were down 4.2% after Nvidia cut its stake in the chip designer by 44%.

Applied Materials, the semiconductor equipment manufacturer, reported fiscal first-quarter adjusted earnings and revenue that beat analysts' expectations but issued mixed guidance, sending the stock down 5.8%. Applied Materials said it expects second-quarter adjusted earnings of $2.12 to $2.48 a share on revenue of $6.7 billion to $7.5 billion, the midpoint of which missed analysts' expectations of $7.21 billion.

Fiscal second-quarter earnings at cybersecurity company Palo Alto Networks were better than Wall Street estimates, but the stock was down 5.5% after Palo Alto estimated third-quarter revenue of between $2.26 billion to $2.29 billion versus forecasts of $2.27 billion, and maintained its full-year outlook for remaining performance obligations.

Coinbase Global reported fourth-quarter earnings of $4.68 a share, easily beating Wall Street forecasts of $2.11. Revenue in the quarter was $2.27 billion, better than estimates of $1.84 billion. Coinbase said trading volume in the period was $439 billion, up from $154 billion in the fourth quarter of 2023. Shares of Coinbase have risen about 40% since Donald Trump's presidential election victory, with the president promising to make the U.S. the "crypto capital of the world" by ushering in a lighter regulatory regime. The stock was down 6.5%.

DraftKings rose 14% after the online sports betting company reported a wider-than-expected fourth-quarter loss but said it was raising its revenue guidance for 2025 to between $6.3 billion to $6.6 billion, compared with its previous outlook of $6.2 billion to $6.6 billion.

Roku jumped 12%. The streaming company reported a narrower-than-expected fourth-quarter loss and revenue of $1.2 billion that topped analysts' expectations. Streaming households at the end of the quarter were 89.8 million, better than consensus of 89 million. Roku said it sees first-quarter revenue of $1.01 billion, up 14% from a year earlier, and full-year revenue of $4.61 billion. Both forecasts were in line with Wall Street expectations. Roku also said it expects to be operating income positive in 2026.

GameStop rose 5.2% after CNBC reported the video game retailer was considering investing in Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies. The report cited sources familiar with the matter.

Informatica slumped 30% after the provider of cloud-based data management software said it expects first-quarter revenue of $380 million to $400 million, below analysts' expectations of $412 million. For the full year, Informatica expects revenue of $1.67 billion to $1.72 billion, below estimates of $1.78 billion.

Twilio declined 16% after the communication-tools provider reported a narrower fourth-quarter loss but issued an outlook that missed expectations. Twilio said it expects first-quarter adjusted earnings of 88 cents to 93 cents a share on revenue of $1.13 billion to $1.14 billion. Analysts had been expecting adjusted earnings of 99 cents a share on revenue of $1.14 billion.

DaVita was down 12% after the dialysis provider said it expects adjusted earnings of $10.20 to $11.30 a share in 2025, below analysts' expectations of $11.38. Separately, Berkshire Hathaway sold $31.7 million of DaVita stock earlier this week. Berkshire now holds 35.9 million shares of DaVita worth about $6.4 billion, a 45% stake.

Write to Joe Woelfel at joseph.woelfel@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: USDCHF enters critical zone as selling pressure mounts
data.description:
USDCHF technicals

The USDCHF is experiencing a sharp decline for the second consecutive day. Despite Swiss CPI inflation coming in lower than expected at -0.1% MoM and 0.4% YoY, while US CPI and PPI showed stronger inflation, the pair has moved lower instead of higher—suggesting that other factors, such as geopolitical risks, political developments, and technical breaks, are influencing price action.

This week, the 100/200-hour moving averages and the 100/200-bar moving averages on the 4-hour chart were all breached, reinforcing the downside momentum. Today, USDCHF is down -0.55%, reaching a session low of 0.8971, which tested a key swing area between 0.8965 and 0.8974 (see green numbered circles on the chart). Currently, the price is modestly above that level at 0.8981, making this zone a key support area where traders may look to buy with a stop below.

If the price breaks below 0.8965, further downside momentum could push the pair toward the next swing area at 0.8914–0.8922. A deeper break below that would shift focus to the 38.2% retracement of the September 2024 rally at 0.8882, followed by the 100-day moving average at 0.8864.

The key question heading into the weekly close is whether the bearish momentum can sustain itself and drive the pair lower. Buyers will be looking to defend current support, while sellers will be eyeing a confirmed break for another leg down. This remains a crucial technical level for both sides.

data.title: Segro Looks to Data Centers as Pandemic-Era Warehouse Boom Fades — Interview
data.description:
By Nina Kienle

U.K. warehouse landlord Segro is looking to expand its presence in the data-center market after a pandemic-era surge in demand for logistics space quietened down, its chief executive said.

A data-center boom driven by artificial intelligence has lifted the fortunes of chip makers, electrical-equipment manufacturers and power suppliers. Landlords like the FTSE 100 real-estate investment trust want to cash in, too.

"We think it's such a profitable opportunity that it's too good to ignore," Segro Chief Executive David Sleath said in an interview. "We've been busy securing power and identifying sites for data-center development."

Sleath said Segro stumbled across the data-center market about two decades ago. Data centers now make up around 8% of the company's revenue, but its opportunities are growing significantly, the CEO said.

"Slough, where the company started 100 years ago, is actually the biggest data-center submarket in Europe. It's the biggest cluster of data centers outside of the U.S.," Sleath said. "We've been building up our knowledge and expertise of the space."

While Segro has been serving the data-center market for about 20 years by building so-called shell facilities--a building's exterior--, it is now looking to build fully fitted data centers with all of the necessary infrastructure through collaborations. Sleath said the investment associated with a fully fitted center is eight or ten times the amount involved in a shell building, but he considers it a profitable use of the company's capital.

"We do have some pricing power and our job is to try and optimize that while giving our customers what they want as well," Sleath said.

Segro's head of data center investment, Andrew Pilsworth, said in a separate interview data-center land is the most valuable land the company has in its portfolio. However, the industry faces numerous challenges amid the rapid expansion. Power constraint is the biggest one, Pilsworth said.

"I think anyone in data centers that says they've got absolutely as much power as they would like to meet the demand is probably not telling the truth," Pilsworth said.

Segro has a land bank with access to 2.3 gigawatts of potential power across 25 European sites, which its CEO said is very significant by anybody's standards.

The company swung to a pretax profit in 2024 to 636 million pounds ($799.2 million) compared with a pretax loss of 263 million pounds the prior year. Net rental income rose to 628 million pounds from 587 million pounds, driven by strong like-for-like rental growth of 5.8% and development completions.

One-third of Segro's portfolio is big-box warehouses with the pandemic years resulting in high demand for this space. However, recent macroeconomic and geopolitical conditions have accelerated the normalization of this trend.

"The last few years, particularly during the pandemic, saw a real surge in demand for new facilities, particularly automated facilities, and that quietened down," Sleath said.

  • Anthony O. Goriainoff contributed to this article.

Write to Nina Kienle at nina.kienle@wsj.com

data.title: EURO STOXX 50 Index Ends the Week 3.15% Higher at 5493.40 — Data Talk
data.description:
The EURO STOXX 50 Index is up 168.00 points or 3.15% this week to 5493.40

  • Largest one week point and percentage gain since the week ending Jan 17, 2025
  • Up for six consecutive weeks
  • Up 621.95 points or 12.77% over the last six weeks
  • Largest six week point gain since the week ending Nov. 2, 2001
  • Largest six week percentage gain since the week ending Dec. 2, 2022
  • Longest winning streak since the week ending March 28, 2024 when the market rose for 10 straight weeks
  • Up seven of the past eight weeks
  • Today it is down 7.10 points or 0.13%
  • Second highest close in history
  • Largest one day point and percentage decline since Friday, Feb. 7, 2025
  • Snaps a four trading day winning streak
  • Off 0.13% from its record close of 5500.50 hit Thursday, Feb. 13, 2025
  • Today's closing value is the second highest this year
  • Off 0.13% from its 52-week high of 5500.50 hit Thursday, Feb. 13, 2025
  • Up 20.16% from its 52-week low of 4571.60 hit Monday, Aug. 5, 2024
  • Rose 15.27% from 52 weeks ago
  • Off 0.13% from its 2025 closing high of 5500.50 hit Thursday, Feb. 13, 2025
  • Up 12.77% from its 2025 closing low of 4871.45 hit Friday, Jan. 3, 2025
  • Month-to-date it is up 3.91%

data.title: GOODYEAR TIRE & RUBBER CO /OH/ SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company, a global leader in tire manufacturing and distribution, has released its 2024 Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. This summary highlights the key aspects of Goodyear's performance and strategic direction as outlined in the report.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Sales: $18,878 million, a decrease of 5.9% from $20,066 million in 2023, primarily due to lower tire volume and global declines in price and product mix.
  • Operating Income: $1,318 million, an increase of $350 million from $968 million in 2023, driven by benefits from the Goodyear Forward plan and lower raw material costs.
  • Net Income: $70 million, compared to a net loss of $689 million in 2023, mainly due to lower rationalization charges, higher segment operating income, and lower impairment charges.
  • Net Income Per Share: $0.24, compared to a net loss per share of $2.42 in 2023, reflecting improved financial performance.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company operates through three regional segments: Americas, Europe, Middle East and Africa (EMEA), and Asia Pacific. Each segment focuses on the development, manufacture, distribution, and sale of tires and related products.
  • Geographical Performance: In 2024, the Americas was the largest segment in terms of revenue, with significant operations in the United States, Canada, Mexico, and South America. EMEA and Asia Pacific also contributed to the company's global footprint, with manufacturing facilities and sales operations across Europe, the Middle East, Africa, and Asia.
  • Sales Units: The company sold 166.6 million tire units worldwide in 2024, a decrease from 173.3 million units in 2023. The Americas segment sold 81.6 million units, EMEA sold 48.9 million units, and Asia Pacific sold 36.1 million units.
  • New Product Launches: In 2024, the company launched several new consumer and commercial tires across its brands, including the Goodyear Assurance WeatherReady 2, Goodyear ElectricDrive 2, and Cooper Discoverer Stronghold AT in the Americas. EMEA introduced the Goodyear Eagle F1 Asymmetric 6 and Dunlop All Season 2, while Asia Pacific released the Goodyear Wrangler Boulder MT and Mickey Thompson Baja Boss A/T.
  • Future Outlook: The company expects to continue its Goodyear Forward transformation plan, aiming for significant margin expansion and cost reductions by the end of 2025. It anticipates benefits from this plan to contribute approximately $750 million to segment operating income in 2025.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Goodyear Forward Transformation Plan: Goodyear announced the Goodyear Forward transformation plan aimed at optimizing its product portfolio, expanding segment operating margins, and reducing leverage to drive long-term shareholder value. This includes a strategic review of chemical operations, the Dunlop brand, and the global OTR tire business.
  • Divestitures and Agreements: The company completed the sale of its OTR tire business to Yokohama for $905 million and entered into a Dunlop Purchase Agreement with SRI for $526 million.
  • Capital Management: Goodyear had $810 million in cash and cash equivalents and $3,555 million of unused credit availability as of December 31, 2024. The company plans capital expenditures of approximately $950 million in 2025 and expects rationalization payments of about $400 million. Goodyear also called for the redemption of its 9.5% Senior Notes due 2025, following the sale of its OTR tire business.
  • Future Outlook: In 2025, Goodyear expects to achieve $750 million in benefits from the Goodyear Forward plan, with a focus on improving operating margins and reducing costs. The company anticipates raw material headwinds but plans to offset these through pricing actions and cost-saving initiatives. Goodyear also expects working capital to be a source of cash flow, contributing $100 million to $150 million in operating cash flows for the year.

Challenges and Risks

  • Implementation Risks: The success of the Goodyear Forward plan is contingent on overcoming challenges such as management focus, retention of key personnel, and unforeseen implementation costs. Additionally, the sale of the Dunlop brand and the OTR tire business introduces risks related to the separation of these businesses and the realization of expected benefits.
  • Market and Competitive Risks: Goodyear faces significant global competition, particularly from Bridgestone and Michelin, and must innovate to maintain market share. Economic downturns, inflation, and supply chain disruptions pose additional risks, potentially impacting raw material costs and operational efficiency.
  • Regulatory Risks: Compliance with environmental laws and potential changes in tariffs and trade agreements could affect the company's global operations.
  • Operational Risks: Management is focused on executing the Goodyear Forward plan to drive shareholder value, but acknowledges the risks associated with strategic initiatives and market conditions. The company is investing in capital improvements to modernize facilities and increase production of higher-margin tires, but these efforts may be disrupted by economic uncertainties and competitive pressures. Goodyear is also addressing labor relations challenges, particularly with unionized employees, which could impact operations if not managed effectively.
  • Market Risks: Goodyear is exposed to market risks such as foreign currency fluctuations, which have negatively impacted sales and operating income. The company also faces interest rate risks due to its variable rate debt, which could increase debt service obligations if rates rise. Additionally, geopolitical events, such as the conflict in Ukraine, have exacerbated supply chain disruptions and increased costs, posing further risks to the company's financial performance.

SEC Filing: GOODYEAR TIRE & RUBBER CO /OH/ [ GT ] - 10-K - Feb. 14, 2025

data.title: EASTMAN CHEMICAL CO SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Eastman Chemical Co, a global specialty materials company, has released its annual Form 10-K report, detailing its financial and operational performance for the fiscal year. The report highlights the company's strategic initiatives, business achievements, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

Eastman Chemical Co reported a solid financial performance for the fiscal year, with key metrics showing growth and stability:

  • Sales: $9,382 million, a 2% increase compared to 2023, driven by higher sales volume despite lower selling prices.
  • Gross Profit: $2,290 million, an 11% increase from 2023, attributed to higher sales volume and lower raw material and energy costs.
  • Earnings before interest and taxes (EBIT): $1,278 million, a slight decrease of 2% from 2023, primarily due to restructuring activities and other charges.
  • Net earnings attributable to Eastman: $905 million, reflecting a slight increase from $894 million in 2023.
  • Diluted EPS: $7.67, up from $7.49 in 2023, indicating improved earnings per share performance.

Business Highlights

Eastman Chemical Co's business performance was marked by growth in several key segments and regions:

  • Revenue Segments:
    • The Advanced Materials segment saw a 4% increase in sales revenue, driven by higher sales volume, particularly in premium interlayers products.
    • The Additives & Functional Products segment experienced a 1% increase in sales revenue, attributed to higher sales volume in agriculture, personal care, aviation, and water treatment markets.
    • The Chemical Intermediates segment's sales revenue remained relatively unchanged, with lower selling prices offset by higher sales volume.
    • The Fibers segment reported a 2% increase in sales revenue, primarily due to higher selling prices in the acetate tow product line.
  • Geographical Performance: Sales revenue in the Asia Pacific region increased by 6%, driven by higher sales volume, particularly within the Advanced Materials segment.
  • New Product Launches:
    • The Advanced Materials segment launched Saflex™ LiteCarbon Clear and Saflex Evoca™, enhancing the sustainability and design potential of laminated glass and electric vehicle glazing.
    • The Additives & Functional Products segment introduced Eastman Esmeri™, a biodegradable cellulosic biopolymer for personal care, and Solus™, a biodegradable paper coating additive for food-safe packaging.
    • The Additives & Functional Products segment launched electronic grade isopropyl alcohol (IPA) for U.S. semiconductor manufacturers, expanding the EastaPure™ electronic chemicals line.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue leveraging its innovation-driven growth model, focusing on sustainable growth through differentiated products and significant scale advantages in R&D and global market access.

Strategic Initiatives

Eastman Chemical Co is actively pursuing strategic initiatives to drive long-term growth and financial stability:

  • Innovation-Driven Growth: The company is focusing on molecular recycling technologies and differentiated application development, with significant investments in manufacturing capabilities in Europe, Middle East, Africa, and Asia Pacific.
  • Capital Management: Eastman has a $1.50 billion revolving credit facility, extended to February 2029. The company repaid $550 million in term loans in 2024 and repurchased 3,001,409 shares of common stock for $300 million under a $2.5 billion authorization. A cash dividend of $0.83 per share was declared for the first quarter of 2025.
  • Future Outlook: Expected capital spending between $700 million and $800 million in 2025, with a focus on maintaining a solid investment-grade credit rating to support long-term strategy and financial flexibility.

Challenges and Risks

Eastman Chemical Co faces several challenges and risks that could impact its business operations and financial performance:

  • Volatility in Raw Material and Energy Prices: Significant fluctuations in these costs pose a risk to the company's profitability. Eastman employs pricing and hedging strategies to mitigate these risks.
  • Environmental Costs and Liabilities: Changes in regulations or unanticipated government enforcement actions could result in higher costs, impacting the company's financial condition.
  • Pension and Postretirement Benefit Obligations: These obligations are influenced by discount rates, expected returns on plan assets, and demographic assumptions, which could lead to significant changes in expenses.
  • Market Risks: Eastman is exposed to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, commodity price changes, and interest rate volatility. The company manages these risks through hedging strategies and derivative contracts.

SEC Filing: EASTMAN CHEMICAL CO [ EMN ] - 10-K - Feb. 14, 2025

data.title: Southern Michigan Bancorp, Inc. Announces Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Earnings
data.description:
COLDWATER, Mich., Feb. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Southern Michigan Bancorp, Inc. (OTC Pink: SOMC) announced fourth quarter net income of $2,650,000, or $0.57 per share, compared to net income of $2,437,000, or $0.54 per share, for the fourth quarter of 2023. Southern earned $10,402,000 or $2.28 per share, for the year ended December 31, 2024, compared with $10,905,000 or $2.40 per share, for the same period one year ago.

John R. Waldron, President and Chief Executive Officer of Southern Michigan Bancorp, Inc., stated, “2024 was another solid year with total assets reaching approximately $1.5 billion. During the year ended December 31, 2024, total loans and deposits grew to $1.116 billion and $1.252 billion, respectively. While our earnings continue to be impacted by the current interest rate environment, we remain encouraged by the strength of our core deposits and our ability to maintain asset quality.”

The allowance for credit losses totaled $12,782,000, or 1.14% of loans on December 31, 2024, compared to $11,697,000, or 1.13% on December 31, 2023. Net loan charge-offs totaled $27,000 for 2024, compared to $15,000 for 2023. Non-performing loans as a percentage of total loans were 0.08% on December 31, 2024 and December 31, 2023.

The annualized return on average assets for the years ended December 31, 2024, and December 31, 2023, was 0.71% and 0.80%, respectively. The annualized return on average equity was 10.07% for 2024 compared to 11.94% for 2023. The tax equivalent net interest margin for the years ending December 31, 2024, and 2023 was 2.98% and 3.16%, respectively.

Southern Michigan Bancorp, Inc. is a bank holding company and the parent company of Southern Michigan Bank & Trust. It operates 18 offices within Branch, Calhoun, Hillsdale, Jackson, Kalamazoo and St. Joseph Counties providing a broad range of consumer, business and wealth management services throughout the region.

This press release contains forward-looking statements that are based on management’s beliefs, assumptions, current expectations, estimates and projections about the financial services industry, the economy, and Southern Michigan Bancorp, Inc. Forward-looking statements are identifiable by words or phrases such as “expected,” “begin,” and other similar words or expressions. All statements with reference to a future time period are forward-looking. Management’s determination of the provision and allowance for credit losses and other accounting estimates, such as the carrying value of goodwill, other real estate owned, mortgage servicing rights and the fair value of investment securities, involves judgments that are inherently forward-looking. The future effect of changes in the financial and credit markets and the national and regional economy on the banking industry, generally, and Southern Michigan Bancorp, Inc., specifically, are also inherently uncertain. These statements are not guarantees of future performance and involve certain risks, uncertainties and assumptions ("risk factors") that are difficult to predict with regard to timing, extend, likelihood and degree of occurrence. Therefore, actual results and outcomes may materially differ from what may be expressed in or implied by such forward-looking statements. Southern Michigan Bancorp, Inc. does not undertake to update forward-looking statements to reflect the impact of circumstances or events that may arise after the date of the forward-looking statements.
